title: Define Date and Time Properties for Languages
breadcrumbs: Define Date and Time Properties for Languages
text: Define Date and Time Properties for Languages The date and time are often displayed differently in different languages. Note: You can also control the formatting of date and time using $NLS_FORMAT and $NLS_LOCALE . For more information, see Language and Locale . Choose More Editors > Date-time Properties . Select or create the message library to hold the language setup. Enter the names of the months in the language for which you are creating the language setup. Enter the days of the week in the language for which you are creating the language setup. Note: Day 1 is Monday. Specify a three-letter abbreviation for each day or month. It is advisable to use the International Standards Organization (ISO) standards. For example, SEP is the standard abbreviation for SEPTEMBER . Specify the default date, time, and date time display formats. Click >> to select from a list of display formats. The display format affects how Uniface handles date and time information in ProcScript. If you do not specify a default, Uniface uses the following default formats: DD-mmm-YYYY for date. hh:nn:ss for time. dd-mmm-yyhh:nn:ss for date and time. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
